pullRequest.title: ci: add Mac Intel (x86_64) build support
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #25.

Limitations: Py 313 for Mac Intel still not supported. Since Pytorch doesn't have a prebuilt wheel for this. And Mac Intel users are highly unlikely to use python 3.13, so let's skip this.

pullRequest.title: fix: add clear error for Ollama dimension mismatch
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #31

Ollama sometimes returns embeddings with inconsistent dimensions, causing cryptic numpy errors. 

Now provides clear error message with troubleshooting steps instead of crashing.

Before: ValueError: inhomogeneous shape
After: Clear message showing which embeddings have wrong dimensions + fix suggestions

pullRequest.title: feat: Add macOS 15 support for M4 Mac compatibility
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

This PR adds macOS 15 support to the CI builds to fix compatibility issues with Mac M4 systems.

### Changes Made
- ✅ **Added macOS 15 CI builds**: New build matrix includes `macos-15` for Python 3.9-3.13
- ✅ **Updated deployment targets**: Increased `MACOSX_DEPLOYMENT_TARGET` from 11.0/13.3 to 14.0 for broader compatibility
- ✅ **Better wheel compatibility**: Generated wheels will now work on macOS 15 systems

### Problem Solved
Fixes issue #34 where Mac M4 users couldn't install leann due to missing wheel compatibility:
- `leann-backend-hnsw>=0.1.1 has no wheels with a matching platform tag (e.g., macosx_15_0_x86_64)`
- Backend registration failures on M4 systems

### User Impact
- Mac M4 users can now install leann without workarounds
- Better compatibility with the latest macOS versions
- Maintains backward compatibility with older macOS versions

### Test Plan
- [x] CI builds pass for all platforms
- [x] Wheel generation works for macOS 15
- [x] No breaking changes to existing functionality

Closes #34

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

pullRequest.title: feat: Add MCP integration support for Slack and Twitter
pullRequest.body:
- Implement SlackMCPReader for connecting to Slack MCP servers
- Implement TwitterMCPReader for connecting to Twitter MCP servers
- Add SlackRAG and TwitterRAG applications with full CLI support
- Support live data fetching via Model Context Protocol (MCP)
- Add comprehensive documentation and usage examples
- Include connection testing capabilities with --test-connection flag
- Add standalone tests for core functionality
- Update README with detailed MCP integration guide
- Add Aakash Suresh to Active Contributors

Resolves #36 

## What does this PR do?

This PR adds comprehensive MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration support to LEANN, enabling users to perform RAG on live data from various platforms through standardized MCP servers. The implementation includes robust readers for Slack messages and Twitter bookmarks, complete RAG applications with CLI support, and extensive documentation.

**Key Features:**

**Slack MCP Integration:**
- **SlackMCPReader**: Connects to Slack MCP servers to fetch live message data with proper authentication handling
- **SlackRAG**: Full RAG application with configurable workspace filtering, channel selection, and conversation grouping
- Connection testing with `--test-connection` flag for easy debugging

**Twitter MCP Integration:**
- **TwitterMCPReader**: Connects to Twitter MCP servers to fetch bookmark data with engagement metadata support
- **TwitterRAG**: Complete RAG application with bookmark filtering, content processing options, and metadata inclusion
- Flexible processing modes for content-only or metadata-rich indexing

**Comprehensive Documentation:**
- Updated README with detailed MCP integration guide including setup instructions and troubleshooting
- Usage examples for both Slack and Twitter integrations
- Expandable sections with example queries and platform extension guidelines

**Testing & Examples:**
- `examples/mcp_integration_demo.py`: Interactive demonstration script showing MCP capabilities
- `tests/test_mcp_standalone.py`: Comprehensive standalone tests verifying core functionality without dependencies
- All tests pass successfully with proper error handling and edge case coverage

**Live Data Benefits:**
- Real-time data access without manual exports
- Standardized protocol works with any MCP-compatible server
- Easy extension framework for adding new platforms
- Secure authentication handled by MCP servers

**Usage Examples:**

```bash
# Test Slack MCP connection
python -m apps.slack_rag --mcp-server "slack-mcp-server" --test-connection

# Index Slack messages
python -m apps.slack_rag \
  --mcp-server "slack-mcp-server" \
  --workspace-name "my-team" \
  --channels general dev-team \
  --query "What did we decide about the product launch?"

# Index Twitter bookmarks
python -m apps.twitter_rag \
  --mcp-server "twitter-mcp-server" \
  --max-bookmarks 1000 \
  --query "What AI articles did I bookmark?"
```

## Related Issues

Fixes #36

## Checklist

- [x] Tests pass (`uv run pytest`) - Created and ran comprehensive standalone tests for all MCP functionality
- [x] Code formatted (`ruff format` and `ruff check`) - All linting issues resolved, follows PEP 8 conventions
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass (`pre-commit run --all-files`) - Code quality checks completed for all new modules

pullRequest.title: Feature/imessage&agent memory  rag support
pullRequest.body:
This PR adds comprehensive RAG support for multiple conversation and chat platforms to LEANN, enabling users to search through their entire conversation history using semantic search. This includes ChatGPT conversations, Claude conversations, and iMessage history - transforming personal conversation data into searchable knowledge bases.

**Key Features:**

**ChatGPT RAG Support:**
- **ChatGPTReader**: Parses ChatGPT HTML/ZIP exports into structured conversations
- Handles both individual HTML files and ZIP archives
- Preserves conversation titles, timestamps, and message roles
- Supports concatenated conversations for better context

**Claude RAG Support:**
- **ClaudeReader**: Parses Claude JSON/ZIP exports into structured conversations  
- Processes JSON files and ZIP archives containing conversation data
- Maintains conversation metadata and message threading
- Flexible message processing options

**iMessage RAG Support:**
- **IMessageReader**: Parses macOS Messages database (`~/Library/Messages/chat.db`) with proper Cocoa timestamp conversion and contact formatting
- **IMessageRAG**: Full RAG application with configurable chunking and conversation grouping options
- Handles both group chats and individual conversations
- Rich metadata preservation (chat names, participants, timestamps, message counts)

**Privacy-first**: All processing happens locally with no data leaving the device

**Comprehensive documentation**: Setup instructions, export guides, troubleshooting tips, and usage examples in README for all platforms

**Flexible processing**: Choose between concatenated conversations or individual message processing across all platforms

**Usage Examples:**

```bash
# ChatGPT conversations
python -m apps.chatgpt_rag --export-path chatgpt_export.html --query "What did I ask about Python?"

# Claude conversations  
python -m apps.claude_rag --export-path claude_export.json --query "What debugging advice did Claude give me?"

# iMessage history (requires Full Disk Access)
python -m apps.imessage_rag --query "What did we discuss about vacation plans?"

# Custom database path for iMessage
python -m apps.imessage_rag --db-path /path/to/backup/chat.db

# Process individual messages instead of conversations
python -m apps.imessage_rag --no-concatenate-conversations
```

## Related Issues

Fixes #40 (ChatGPT RAG support)
Fixes #100 (Claude RAG support)  
Fixes #126 (iMessage RAG support)

## Checklist

- [x] Tests pass (`uv run pytest`) - Created and ran comprehensive tests for all readers: ChatGPT HTML parsing, Claude JSON parsing, iMessage database parsing, conversation grouping, and message processing
- [x] Code formatted (`ruff format` and `ruff check`) - All linting issues resolved across all implementations, follows PEP 8 naming conventions
- [x] Pre-commit hooks pass (`pre-commit run --all-files`) - Code quality checks completed for all new modules

pullRequest.title: Docs/Core: Low-Resource Setups, SkyPilot Option, and No-Recompute
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #43.

---

### **Key Changes**

* **Documentation:** Clarified and added support for low-resource setups, including:
    * OpenAI embeddings
    * SkyPilot remote build
    * `--no-recompute` usage
* **CLI:** Added more intuitive flags:
    * `--no-recompute` (for `build`/`search`/`ask`)
* **HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World):** If `is_recompute=False` and `is_compact=True`, the process will now automatically force a non-compact build and issue a warning for compatibility.
* **Index Portability:**
    * The index metadata now keeps relative path entries.
    * Added optional `path_relative` and `index_path_relative` hints for better portability.
* **Reader:** The `PassageManager` can now resolve absolute paths that don't exist by falling back to relative path hints or sibling filenames, which is a non-destructive operation.
* **Embedding Server:** Path resolution is now delegated to the `PassageManager` via `metadata_file_path` to ensure consistent behavior across the system.
* **SkyPilot Integration:**
    * Fixed environment variable configurations.
    * Added tunable parameters.
    * Ensured C++ runtime libraries are installed.
    * Made sure the remote index directory exists before use.
    * Documented the `rsync` path for transferring data.

---

### **Verification**

* Successfully built an index remotely on AWS using SkyPilot.
* Synced the index back to a local machine using `rsync`.
* Confirmed that local search works correctly (with recompute).
* Confirmed that the `--no-recompute` flag works as expected when building a non-compact HNSW index.

pullRequest.title: Add Google Gemini API support for chat and embeddings
pullRequest.body:
Resolves #49 

## Summary

Add comprehensive support for Google Gemini API in LEANN, including both chat generation and text embeddings.

• **GeminiChat class**: Support for latest `gemini-2.5-flash` model with configurable parameters (temperature, max_tokens, top_p)
• **Gemini embeddings**: Support for `text-embedding-004` model with batch processing and progress tracking
• **Factory integration**: Added "gemini" type to `get_llm()` factory function
• **Documentation**: Updated API docs to include Gemini embedding mode
• **Error handling**: Proper API key validation and detailed error messages

## Features Added

### Chat Generation
- New `GeminiChat` class implementing `LLMInterface`
- Uses latest `gemini-2.5-flash` model (replacing deprecated `gemini-1.5-flash`)
- Supports standard parameters: `temperature`, `max_tokens`, `top_p`
- Proper error handling with detailed failure messages

### Text Embeddings
- New `compute_embeddings_gemini()` function
- Uses `text-embedding-004` model with 768-dimensional vectors
- Batch processing support (100 texts per batch)
- Progress bar integration with tqdm
- Client caching for performance

### Integration
- Added "gemini" type to embedding mode options
- Updated `get_llm()` factory to support Gemini models
- Environment variable support: `GEMINI_API_KEY`

## Usage Examples

**Chat:**
```python
from leann.chat import get_llm

llm = get_llm({
    "type": "gemini",
    "model": "gemini-2.5-flash",
    "api_key": "your_api_key"
})

response = llm.ask("Hello, how are you?")
```

**Embeddings:**
```python
from leann.api import LeannBuilder

builder = LeannBuilder(
    backend_name="hnsw",
    embedding_model="text-embedding-004",
    embedding_mode="gemini"
)
```

## Test Results

All functionality tested successfully:
- ✅ Chat generation working with gemini-2.5-flash
- ✅ Embeddings working with text-embedding-004 (768 dimensions)
- ✅ Integration with LEANN API working
- ✅ Error handling and validation working

pullRequest.title: Fix restart embedding server when passages change
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #113 

- Track filesystem signatures for the index metadata and passage shard files so the embedding server restarts whenever those assets change; this keeps
PassageManager in sync with incremental indexing updates.
- Propagate the richer configuration signature through all embedding-server launch paths (desktop + Colab) so restart logic is consistent.
- Add a regression test that stubs process management and verifies the server is reused when metadata is unchanged and restarted after passage data
updates.

Testing

  - `python -m pytest tests/test_embedding_server_manager.py`

pullRequest.title: feat: Add support for configurable local LLM endpoints
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
Adds support for configurable local LLM service endpoints, allowing LEANN to work with various local services beyond Ollama's default endpoint.

Fixes #114

## Changes
- Add centralized configuration module (`settings.py`) for resolving endpoints from environment variables
- Support custom hosts for Ollama-compatible services via `--host` CLI parameter  
- Support OpenAI-compatible local services via `--api-base` and `--api-key` parameters
- Pass embedding provider options through the entire pipeline (CLI → Builder → Server)
- Update both LLM chat and embedding services to use configurable endpoints

## Environment Variables
The following environment variables are now supported:
- `LEANN_OLLAMA_HOST`, `OLLAMA_HOST`, `LOCAL_LLM_ENDPOINT` - for Ollama-compatible services
- `LEANN_OPENAI_BASE_URL`, `OPENAI_BASE_URL` - for OpenAI-compatible services
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` - for API key authentication

## Example Usage
```bash
# Use MSTY Studio endpoint
leann ask my-index "What is LEANN?" --llm ollama --host http://localhost:10000

# Use LM Studio's OpenAI-compatible endpoint  
leann ask my-index "What is LEANN?" --llm openai --api-base http://localhost:1234/v1

# Build index with custom embedding endpoint
leann build my-index --docs ./docs --embedding-mode ollama --embedding-host http://localhost:10000
```

## Testing
Tests pass locally with `uv run --with '.[dev]' pytest`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

pullRequest.title: security: Enhance Hugging Face model loading security - resolves #136
pullRequest.body:
- Set trust_remote_code=False by default in HFChat class
- Add explicit trust_remote_code parameter to HFChat.__init__()
- Add security warning when trust_remote_code=True is used
- Update get_llm() function to support trust_remote_code parameter
- Update benchmark utilities (load_hf_model, load_vllm_model, load_qwen_vl_model)
- Add comprehensive documentation for security implications

Security Benefits:
- Prevents arbitrary code execution from compromised model repositories
- Requires explicit opt-in for models that need remote code execution
- Shows clear warnings when security is reduced
- Follows security-by-default principle

Migration Guide:
- Most users: No changes needed (more secure by default)
- Users with models requiring remote code: Add trust_remote_code=True explicitly
- Config users: Add 'trust_remote_code': true to LLM config if needed

Fixes #136

pullRequest.title: Drop Python 3.9 support and modernize macOS runners
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Drop Python 3.9 support and require Python 3.10+
- Migrate from deprecated macOS 13 to `macos-15-intel` (free runner)
- Add macOS 26 (beta) for future compatibility testing
- Fixes https://github.com/yichuan-w/LEANN/issues/137

## Rationale
1. **Python 3.9**: Reached end-of-life (October 2025) and the codebase uses PEP 604 union type syntax (`str | None`) which requires Python 3.10+.
2. **macOS 13**: GitHub Actions deprecated macos-13 runner (Dec 2025). Migrated to `macos-15-intel` which is a **free standard runner** available until Aug 2027.

See: https://github.com/actions/runner-images/issues/13045

## Changes
- Remove Python 3.9 from CI build matrix
- Replace `macos-13` with `macos-15-intel` for Intel Mac builds
- Add `macos-26` (beta) for testing future macOS compatibility
- Update `requires-python` to `>=3.10` in all `pyproject.toml` files
- Update classifiers to reflect supported Python versions (3.10-3.13)

## Platform Support

| Platform | Architecture | Pre-built wheels |
|----------|--------------|-----------------|
| Linux | x86_64 | ✅ |
| Linux | arm64 | ✅ |
| macOS 15+ | Intel (x86_64) | ✅ via macos-15-intel |
| macOS 14+ | Apple Silicon (arm64) | ✅ |

**Note:** Intel Mac wheels require macOS 15+ because the `macos-15-intel` runner builds with macOS 15 system libraries. Users on older Intel Macs (macOS 13-14) can still install from source.

## Build Matrix

| Runner | Architecture | Deployment Target | Python |
|--------|--------------|-------------------|--------|
| ubuntu-22.04 | x86_64 | - | 3.10-3.13 |
| ubuntu-24.04-arm | arm64 | - | 3.10-3.13 |
| macos-14 | arm64 | 14.0 | 3.10-3.13 |
| macos-15 | arm64 | 15.0 | 3.10-3.13 |
| macos-15-intel | x86_64 | 15.0 | 3.10-3.12 ⚠️ |
| macos-26 (beta) | arm64 | 26.0 | 3.10-3.13 |

⚠️ **Intel Mac + Python 3.13**: PyTorch has no wheels for this combination. PyTorch ≤2.4.1 doesn't support Python 3.13, and PyTorch ≥2.5.0 dropped Intel Mac (x86_64) support entirely. Users needing Python 3.13 on Intel Macs can install from source.

## Test plan
- [x] CI passes with Python 3.10+ builds
- [x] All platforms build successfully
- [ ] macOS 26 beta builds complete (may have expected failures)

pullRequest.title: Feature/optimize ollama batching
pullRequest.body:
## What does this PR do?

<!-- Brief description of your changes -->

## Related Issues

Fixes #

## Checklist

- [X] Tests pass (`uv run pytest`): as mentioned in #150 , ast chunking part is waiting to be synced with the fix in 150; diskann is not configure on my mac but this 151 is irrelevant regarding to the diskann backend. Other tests all pass
- [X] Code formatted (`ruff format` and `ruff check`)
- [X] Pre-commit hooks pass (`pre-commit run --all-files`)

 ## Summary

  Fixes #151

  This PR implements true batch processing for Ollama embeddings by migrating from the deprecated `/api/embeddings`
  endpoint to the modern `/api/embed` endpoint.

  > **Note**: This PR is based on `feature/add-metadata-output` and includes changes from PR #150 (metadata output + ZMQ
  fixes). The Ollama batching optimization is in the latest commit (8bb7743). PR #150 can be merged first, then this PR
  will cleanly apply the batching changes only.

  ## Commits in this PR

  1. `d6a3c28` - feat: add metadata output to search results (from #150)
  2. `76e1633` - fix: resolve ZMQ linking issues (from #150)
  3. `5073f31` - style: apply ruff formatting (from #150)
  4. `8bb7743` - **feat: implement true batch processing for Ollama embeddings** ⭐ (NEW)

  ## Changes (Ollama Batching)

  - **Endpoint migration**: `/api/embeddings` → `/api/embed`
  - **Parameter update**: `"prompt"` (single text) → `"input"` (array of texts)
  - **Response parsing**: Updated to handle batch embeddings array
  - **Timeout**: Increased to 60s for batch processing
  - **Error handling**: Improved for batch request failures

  ## Performance Impact

  ### Before
  - Made 32 separate API calls per "batch" of 32 texts
  - Significant HTTP connection/handshake overhead
  - Used deprecated API endpoint

  ### After
  - Makes 1 API call per batch of 32 texts
  - Reduces HTTP overhead by 32×
  - Uses modern, recommended API endpoint

  ### Actual Results (tested on 2,374 chunks)
  - API calls reduced from ~76K to ~75 requests
  - Batch size: 32 for MPS/CPU, 128 for CUDA
  - Processing time: Moderate improvement from reduced HTTP overhead

  ## Important Note

  Through empirical testing on Ollama v0.12.6, we confirmed that Ollama processes embedding batch items **sequentially**
  (not in parallel). The performance improvement comes from reduced HTTP overhead, not GPU parallelization.

  For users requiring maximum performance, we recommend e.g. :
  ```bash
  --embedding-mode sentence-transformers --embedding-model Alibaba-NLP/gte-Qwen2-1.5B-instruct

  This provides true GPU batch parallelization and is 10-20× faster than Ollama for large embedding workloads.

  Testing

  Tested with:
  - 2,374 chunks, avg 4,610 chars each
  - Ollama v0.12.6
  - Model: qwen3-embedding:0.6b
  - Batch size: 32 texts per batch

  Related Files

  - packages/leann-core/src/leann/embedding_compute.py:570-861

pullRequest.title: Fix/chunking token limit behavior
pullRequest.body:
## Fixes Issue #153: Chunking Token Limit Behavior

This PR addresses the cascade batch failures when using Ollama embeddings with token-limited models (e.g., `nomic-embed-text-v2` with 512 token limit).

### **Root Cause Analysis**
- **Unit mismatch**: Traditional chunking uses tokens, AST chunking uses characters
- **Overlap behavior**: Overlap is added AFTER chunk size, not included in it
- **No token awareness**: No validation against embedding model token limits
- **Cascade failures**: One oversized chunk causes entire batch (32 chunks) to fail

### **Solutions Implemented**

#### **1. Token-Aware Truncation (Critical Fix)**
- Added `EMBEDDING_MODEL_LIMITS` registry for known models
- Implemented `truncate_to_token_limit()` with tiktoken support
- Applied truncation before Ollama API calls to prevent 500 errors

#### **2. CLI Help Text Clarification** 
- Updated all help text to clearly specify **TOKENS** vs **CHARACTERS**
- Added explicit warnings about overlap behavior
- Provided safe recommendations for 512 token models
- Changed AST defaults: 768→300 chars, 96→64 overlap (safer)

#### **3. Post-Chunking Validation**
- Added `validate_chunk_token_limits()` with real token counting
- Implemented safety net that validates all chunks ≤ 512 tokens
- Added warnings when AST parameters might exceed token limits

#### **4. Improved Batch Error Handling**
- Enhanced error detection for token limit violations  
- Added individual text recovery on batch failures
- Prevented cascade failures from single oversized chunks
- Added exponential backoff for retries

### **Testing**
- [x] No linting errors
- [x] Backward compatibility maintained
- [x] Safe defaults for 512 token models
- [x] Graceful fallbacks when tiktoken unavailable

### **Files Changed**
- `packages/leann-core/src/leann/embedding_compute.py` - Token truncation & batch handling
- `packages/leann-core/src/leann/chunking_utils.py` - Validation & safe calculations  
- `packages/leann-core/src/leann/cli.py` - Help text & safe defaults
- `apps/base_rag_example.py` - Consistent parameter documentation

### **Resolves**
- Eliminates Ollama 500 Server Error batch failures
- Prevents token limit violations for common embedding models  
- Provides clear user guidance for safe parameter settings
- Maintains backward compatibility while adding safety features

Closes #153

pullRequest.title: Fix: Prevent duplicate PDF processing when using --file-types .pdf
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #175

Problem:
When --file-types .pdf is specified, PDFs were being processed twice:
1. Separately with PyMuPDF/pdfplumber extractors
2. Again in the 'other file types' section via SimpleDirectoryReader

This caused duplicate processing and potential conflicts.

Solution:
- Exclude .pdf from other_file_extensions when PDFs are already processed separately
- Only load other file types if there are extensions to process
- Prevents duplicate PDF processing

Changes:
- Added logic to filter out .pdf from code_extensions when loading other file types if PDFs were processed separately
- Updated SimpleDirectoryReader to use filtered extensions
- Added check to skip loading if no other extensions to process

## What does this PR do?

This PR fixes an issue where the "Source:" field in search results was empty when using the `--show-metadata` flag. It ensures that the `source` metadata field is correctly propagated from the document ingestion stage through to the chunking process (both traditional and AST-aware chunking).

pullRequest.title: Feature/linux omp 208
pullRequest.body:
## What does this PR do?

This PR adds Linux-specific default thread limits to avoid FAISS/HNSW + OpenMP oversubscription that can lead to ZmqDistanceComputer hangs and distance batch fetch failed crashes on multi-core CPU-only Linux systems.
Specifically, on Linux we now set (only if the user hasn’t already set them):
- OMP_NUM_THREADS=1
- MKL_NUM_THREADS=1
- FAISS_NUM_THREADS=1
- OMP_WAIT_POLICY=PASSIVE

Fixes #208 

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests pass (`uv run pytest`)
- [ ] Code formatted (`ruff format` and `ruff check`)
- [ ] Pre-commit hooks pass (`pre-commit run --all-files`)

pullRequest.title: fix(diskann): warn when dataset has fewer vectors than PQ centroids
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #280

## Problem

When building a DiskANN index with fewer than 256 vectors, the PQ training
step in DiskANN requests 256 k-means centroids regardless of dataset size.
On Windows with Intel MKL, this causes repeated non-fatal `cblas_sgemm`
parameter errors:

```
Intel MKL ERROR: Parameter 9 was incorrect on entry to cblas_sgemm.
```

The index builds correctly and search works, but the output confuses users
who don't know the errors are harmless.

## Solution

Add a Python-level warning in `DiskannBuilder.build()` that triggers when
`num_vectors < 256` (the default `NUM_PQ_CENTROIDS`). The warning:

- Explains the errors are non-fatal
- Tells users the index will still build correctly
- Suggests `--backend-name hnsw` as a noise-free alternative for small datasets

This is a pure Python change — no C++ submodule modifications needed. A
proper fix at the C++ level (capping `num_centers` in `generate_pq_pivots()`)
remains a future improvement.

## Testing

The warning path is triggered by any DiskANN build with fewer than 256
vectors. No regression risk: existing builds with ≥ 256 vectors are unaffected.

pullRequest.title: test: verify daemon isolation across different passages files (fixes #281)
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #281

## Problem

When multiple DiskANN tests run sequentially in the same process, a daemonized embedding server started by test A could be incorrectly adopted by test B if the config-signature comparison did not include the passages file path and stat information. This caused the server to serve node IDs from index B while having index A's passages loaded, resulting in:

```
ZMQ_FETCH_ERROR: Server response has invalid dimensions size.
RuntimeError: Failed to fetch embeddings
```

## Solution

Added two regression tests to `test_embedding_server_manager.py` that directly exercise this scenario using the existing mock infrastructure (no real subprocesses or network):

- **`test_different_passages_files_start_separate_daemons`**: Two managers pointing to different passages files (simulating sequential pytest runs each in their own temp directory) must each spawn a separate daemon and use distinct ZMQ ports. Without `passages_file`/`passages_signature` in the config-signature hash key, both managers would map to the same registry file and the second would silently adopt the first daemon.

- **`test_same_passages_file_reuses_existing_daemon`**: The positive case — two managers for the *same* passages file should share the existing daemon (no duplicate spawning).

## Testing

```bash
pytest tests/test_embedding_server_manager.py -v
# 13 passed
```

pullRequest.title: feat: re-add web search with robust dual-source routing (#283)
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Re-introduces web search (Serper API) and page fetching (Jina Reader) to the ReAct agent, replacing the reverted PR #232 with robust error handling and dual-source routing.

**Key improvements over the original:**
- Dynamic prompt adapts tool list based on whether `SERPER_API_KEY` is configured
- Graceful fallback when `web_search` is called without an API key (guides LLM to use `leann_search`)
- Error responses from Serper (401, rate limits, etc.) handled without crashing the agent
- `visit_page` errors (404, network failures) caught and surfaced as observations
- `search_history` entries now include a `source` field (`"local"` or `"web"`) for debugging
- CLI gains `--serper-api-key` and `--jina-api-key` flags

**Files changed:**
- `packages/leann-core/src/leann/web_search.py` — re-added with typo fix
- `packages/leann-core/src/leann/react_agent.py` — dual-source routing, dynamic prompts, error handling
- `packages/leann-core/src/leann/cli.py` — new CLI args, source tags in search history display
- `tests/test_react_dual_source.py` — 13 tests covering all scenarios from issue #283

## Test plan

- [x] Prompt includes all 3 tools when Serper key is present
- [x] Prompt shows only `leann_search` when no key
- [x] Local-only routing works correctly
- [x] Web-only routing works correctly (mocked)
- [x] Mixed routing (local → web) works correctly
- [x] Web results properly formatted as observations
- [x] `visit_page` content truncated to 15k chars
- [x] Local search results include scores
- [x] No API key → graceful fallback message, no crash
- [x] Invalid API key → error handled, agent continues
- [x] 404 page → error string, agent continues
- [x] Max iterations with mixed sources → still produces answer
- [x] `search_history` entries include `source` field

All 13 tests pass locally (193s).

Closes #283

pullRequest.title: fix(diskann): surface native build failures instead of silent exits (…
pullRequest.body:
## Summary
- Run DiskANN native build in a child process so native exits/crashes do not terminate the main CLI silently.
- Convert unexpected native termination into explicit Python `RuntimeError` with actionable messaging.
- Validate expected DiskANN output (`*_disk.index`) immediately after native build and fail fast if missing.

## Why
Issue #287 reports `leann build` appearing to finish prematurely on DiskANN, then `leann ask` failing because the collection/index cannot be found. This can happen when native build exits before metadata/index finalization while not surfacing a clear error.

This change ensures the failure is explicit at build time and prevents false-success partial indexes.

## Test plan
- `ruff check packages/leann-backend-diskann/leann_backend_diskann/diskann_backend.py`
- Manual DiskANN build reproduction path (expected now: explicit error instead of silent premature success)

Closes #287
